# WiSo — Skill Arm (live)

**Date:** 2026-06-28 · **Faculty:** WiSo (Politikwissenschaft + Wirtschaftswissenschaft) · **Arm:** SKILL

## Persona (6 dimensions)

- **Interests:** Comparative politics (regimes, democratization, party/welfare systems); political economy
- **Methods:** Quantitative-empirical (regression/panel), comparative case analysis, some text-as-data
- **Domain:** Political institutions / governance / economy–politics interface
- **Thesis style:** Empirical, data-driven analysis
- **Skills:** R/Stata, statistics, survey & panel data
- **No-gos:** No pure normative political theory; no heavy formal/mathematical-proof economics

## Route (search-strategy §2)

Primary: WiSo → Politikwissenschaft (comparative politics) + WiSo → Wirtschaftswissenschaft (political economy).

## Pass 1 — backbone crawl

- **Institut für Politikwissenschaft** Lehrende page → full professor list.
- **FB Wirtschaftswissenschaft** Lehrende/Lehrstühle page → VWL chairs.

## Option Map (grouped by interest dimension)

### Comparative politics

**1. Politics of the Middle East & Comparative Politics — Prof. Dr. Oliver Schlumberger** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/vorderer-orient-und-vergleichende-politikwissenschaft-professor-schlumberger/))
- Relevance: Comparative political-regime research, **democratization & authoritarianism**, good governance, political economy of developing countries — a double hit on comparative politics + political economy.
- Pros/difficulties: Strong comparative-regimes fit; regional (MENA/non-OECD) focus — frame a comparative design that fits.
- Conversation starter: Comparative thesis on authoritarian resilience / democratization indicators.

**2. Comparative Politics & European Integration — Prof. Dr. Gabriele Abels** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Comparative politics + EU governance, gender & democracy.
- Pros/difficulties: Good comparative-institutions fit; EU-heavy.
- Conversation starter: Comparative analysis of representation in EU multilevel governance.

**3. Comparative Public Policy — Prof. Dr. Martin Seeleib-Kaiser** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Comparative welfare-state & social policy — sits at comparative politics ∩ political economy.
- Pros/difficulties: Strong quantitative comparative-policy fit (good for R/panel thesis).
- Conversation starter: Cross-national panel analysis of welfare-state reform.

**4. Comparative Public Policy — Prof. Dr. Cecilia Bruzelius** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Comparative public policy, social policy & mobility — empirical comparative work.
- Conversation starter: Comparative study of social-rights eligibility across EU states.

**5. Greater China Studies / Comparative Politics of Asia — Prof. Dr. Gunter Schubert** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Comparative politics of China/Taiwan, political economy of development.
- Conversation starter: Comparative governance/local-state thesis on China.

### Political economy

**6. Politics & Economy / Political Economy — Prof. Dr. Hans-Jürgen Bieling** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-sozialwissenschaften/politikwissenschaft/institut/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Direct hit — **International Political Economy**, European integration, state-society theory.
- Pros/difficulties: Core political-economy chair; more critical/IPE-theoretical — confirm appetite for a quantitative empirical design.
- Conversation starter: IPE thesis on European economic-governance regimes.

**7. Public Economics — Prof. Dr. Georg Wamser** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/wirtschafts-und-sozialwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/faecher/fachbereich-wirtschaftswissenschaft/wirtschaftswissenschaft/lehrende/))
- Relevance: Public economics / taxation — political-economy-of-policy from the economics side, strongly empirical.
- Pros/difficulties: Quantitative econometrics fit; economics framing (not political science).
- Conversation starter: Empirical thesis on tax-policy responses / firm behavior.

### Adjacent economics (political-economy-relevant, secondary)

- **International Macroeconomics & Finance — Prof. Dr. Gernot Müller**; **International Economics & Labor Markets — Prof. Dr. Frank Stähler**; **Economic History — Prof. Dr. Jörg Baten** (long-run political economy / inequality). All empirical VWL chairs adjacent to political economy.

### Excluded by no-go

- Chair for Ethics of Globalization — Prof. Claus Dierksmeier (normative theory → conflicts with "no pure normative political theory").
- IR / Peace & Conflict (Diez, Hasenclever) — kept as off-core (IR, not comparative politics/PE).

> **Coverage caveat:** Covers publicly visible chairs as of 2026-06-28. Chairs with weak web presence may be missing. To catch them: visit the Politikwissenschaft & Wirtschaftswissenschaft Lehrende pages directly, ask the Fachschaft, and check the Vorlesungsverzeichnis.
